item.title: Does private credit have a credit quality problem?
item.description: The stock market thinks so

item.title: The economic consequences of war with Iran
item.description: There are lasting lessons about energy resilience to be learnt from this conflict

item.title: Is intergenerational inequality unfair?
item.description: Sloppy assumptions do not help Millennials — or their Baby Boomer detractors

item.title: UBS’s new private equity deals confront an old problem
item.description: A firm should think carefully about which risks are worthwhile and tolerable to their clients

item.title: The usefulness of useless knowledge
item.description: Politicians aren’t the best judges of the merits of scientific research

item.title: How to fight deepfakes
item.description: People are now little better than chance at telling what’s real and what’s not

item.title: Rising sulphur prices carry whiff of unintended consequences
item.description: Global efforts to phase out fossil fuels will erode supply of cheap sulphur over time

item.title: How can I get my money out of a third-party account?
item.description: The return on my investment hasn’t materialised and I’m worried there may be fraud on the account

item.title: Ask a stylist: how do you pack for business travel?
item.description: Do your pre-trip homework, focus on neutrals — and take nothing that isn’t multifunctional

item.title: No snow, no refund: weird weather tests Big Ski’s business model
item.description: Vail’s attempt to lock in revenue with its annual pass has come adrift because of rising prices amid less of the white stuff

item.title: There is no easy exit to Trump’s war
item.description: The US and Israeli attacks on Iran will leave the Middle East in greater turmoil

item.title: There is no return to normal on oil any time soon
item.description: Even if the Iran war ends quickly, governments will need to prioritise security of supply

item.title: America has become an agent of chaos in world energy markets
item.description: A succession of US foreign policy choices has destabilised the oil industry

item.title: Taco on Iran will come too late for Trump
item.description: The US president has already done lasting damage to international trust in America

item.title: The thing that everyone expected to happen has happened
item.description: Trump wants the war to be over

item.title: Iran is a crucial test case for the American way of war
item.description: The conflict in Ukraine seemed to cast doubt on a military model based on overwhelming air power

item.title: Berkeley confronts the crisis in London homebuilding
item.description: The FTSE 100 group specialising in brownfield development faces tax and regulatory obstacles

item.title: What the UK should learn from this energy crisis
item.description: There is little doubt that the windfall tax has hit investment in the North Sea

item.title: Britain’s safety net isn’t set up for a widespread jobs shock
item.description: The state does not offer a temporary cushion to support those who were previously in work

item.title: Japan’s playbook on raw materials security is worth taking global
item.description: The country has been ahead of others in heeding the danger of China’s stranglehold on critical minerals

item.title: SoftBank’s bet on OpenAI is starting to weigh
item.description: Japanese group has suffered from recent share falls and a negative outlook from rating agency S&P

item.title: The déjà vu of Europe’s energy shock
item.description: The UK and EU must raise their game on power security

item.title: The new food fad sweeping Korea
item.description: Purchase limits had to be imposed on Dubai chewy cookies

item.title: The UK’s public leadership pipeline has fallen into disrepair
item.description: Institutions are failing to deliver basic commitments because of failures of leadership

item.title: Trump’s Venezuela strategy has failed in Iran
item.description: The appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei has dashed the US president’s hope of picking Iran’s new leader
